assessment: The Quotes sub-tab is functionally legible — tabs, filter, and a clean table with consistent columns — but it fails on its two most important jobs: telling the user the state of their quote pipeline, and distinguishing quotes from orders. The status strip reports Unsent/Sent/Converted all as 0 despite every visible row being DRAFT, and every quote number is prefixed 'ORD-' which will collide cognitively with the Orders tab one click away. There is also a redundant double H1 ('Sales' shell title + 'Sales Quotes' page title) and a search placeholder that claims the Cmd+K shortcut already owned by the global command palette.
notable_strengths: Column set (Quote #, Client, Created, Valid Until, Status, Total) is the right minimum for a quote queue and the row hierarchy is calm.
Status pipeline summary (Saved / Unsent / Sent / Converted) in the header is the correct mental model — it just needs to be accurate.
Mode toggle (Spreadsheet View / Standard View) and the 'New Quote' CTA are right where a sales rep expects them.

findings.title: Pipeline counters omit Draft and contradict the visible table
findings.what_is_wrong: The header strip shows 'Saved / Unsent: 0 / Sent: 0 / Converted: 0' while every one of the ~11 visible rows carries a DRAFT badge. There is no Draft count and the zeros make the pipeline look empty when it is not.
findings.why_it_matters: A sales manager glances at these counters to decide what to work on. Today they read as 'nothing to do' while the queue below is full of unsent drafts — the most important segment to chase.
findings.proposed_fix: Add a leading 'Draft: N' counter before Unsent, and make each counter a filter chip that sets the status filter. Replace the ambiguous green 'Saved' pill with a neutral 'All: N' total. Drive counts from the same `quotes.list` payload that renders the table so they can never diverge.
findings.functionality_preserved: QuotesWorkSurface continues to consume `quotes.*` tRPC output; the fix only re-maps existing status buckets into the header strip and reuses the existing 'All Statuses' filter behavior.

findings.title: Quote rows use an 'ORD-' prefix, indistinguishable from orders
findings.what_is_wrong: Every row in the Quote # column reads 'ORD-000378', 'ORD-000370', etc. The Orders tab sits one click to the left and uses the same prefix pattern.
findings.why_it_matters: Reps routinely paste these numbers into chats and emails ('send me ORD-000378'). When the same identifier space is shared between quotes and orders, support threads and search collide, and converting a quote to an order creates two records with overlapping-looking IDs.
findings.proposed_fix: Display the quote identifier with a `QUO-` (or `Q-`) visual prefix in this column while keeping the underlying stored id unchanged — render as `QUO-000378` with the numeric tail preserved. If backend ids truly are shared with orders, add a small 'Quote' tag next to the number so the row is self-identifying when copy-pasted out of context.
findings.functionality_preserved: Does not touch the `quotes.*` router or the stored order/quote id scheme — purely a column renderer change inside QuotesWorkSurface, so `quoteId` deep-links and conversion flows keep working.

findings.title: Duplicate H1 and a search placeholder that squats the global Cmd+K
findings.what_is_wrong: The page renders two H1s ('Sales' from `LinearWorkspaceShell` and 'Sales Quotes' from the surface), and the in-table search input shows placeholder 'Search quotes… (Cmd+K)' — but Cmd+K is already bound to the global command palette (visible top bar).
findings.why_it_matters: Two H1s flatten the hierarchy and push the useful counters below the fold on short viewports. The Cmd+K hint teaches users a shortcut that will actually open the command palette, eroding trust in in-app hints.
findings.proposed_fix: Demote 'Sales Quotes' to an H2 (or drop it and let the shell title carry 'Sales › Quotes'), and tighten the subtitle row so the counters sit on the same line as the title. Change the search placeholder to 'Search quotes by #, client, or total' and remove the '(Cmd+K)' hint; if a local shortcut is desired, bind '/' and label it '(/)'.
findings.functionality_preserved: LinearWorkspaceShell title/meta-strip API is untouched; only the QuotesWorkSurface internal header markup and input placeholder string change, preserving the `quotes.list` search behavior.

redesign_sketch: One H1 from the shell: 'Sales', breadcrumb 'Sell › Sales › Quotes'. Below it, a single dense header row: left side shows 'Quotes' with a muted total (e.g., '24 quotes') and four clickable status chips (Draft 11 · Sent 6 · Accepted 4 · Expired 3) that drive the status filter; right side holds Spreadsheet/Standard toggle and a primary 'New Quote' button. Filter bar beneath: search input ('Search quotes by #, client, or total'), status select, and a Valid-Until date range. Table columns unchanged but Quote # renders as 'QUO-000378' with a subtle 'Quote' tag, Valid Until cells turn amber when <7 days and red when past, and the trailing chevron opens a right drawer with Convert-to-Order / Duplicate / Send actions so the queue stays keyboard-navigable.
